title: Schistostephium umbellatum
taxonomy: Schistostephium umbellatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a part of the tribe Inuleae, which includes other plants with similar characteristics. The genus Schistostephium is a group of plants native to Africa, with several species found in different regions. The classification of Schistostephium umbellatum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family. Further classification may also include its kingdom and phylum.
uses: Schistostephium umbellatum, also known as the Golden Cassia or Yellow Cassia, has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and flowers are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and other infections. In some African cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments.
